title: Standalone components vs NgModules
description: Standalone components declare their own imports and replace much NgModule boilerplate, changing how features are composed and lazy-loaded.
body: Use when structuring an app or migrating module-based code. NgModules group declarations, imports, and providers and historically were the unit of compilation and lazy loading. Standalone components set standalone: true and list their own imports directly, so they can be used and lazy-loaded without a wrapping module, and apps bootstrap with bootstrapApplication and route-level loadComponent. Do not mix the two inconsistently or assume a standalone component is auto-available without importing it. Actions: import standalone components/directives/pipes where used, and migrate modules incrementally. Validation signals include the app building and a lazy route loading the standalone component. Risk boundary: a half-migrated graph with missing imports fails at compile or runtime; finish or revert a migration rather than leaving it partial.
